workItems.title: Fix: audit_poller persist last_audit only on handler success
workItems.description:
Problem: The audit poller persists last_audit_at BEFORE calling the handler. If the handler fails, the item is skipped for the cooldown window and retries are suppressed.

Decision: Persist last_audit only when the handler completes successfully (handler returns True). This follows the recommended behaviour to avoid hiding failures. Also record last_attempt_at for observability if useful.

Work: Modify poll_and_handoff to persist only on handler success; add last_attempt_at vs last_success_at if helpful; add integration tests that simulate handler failure and success.

Acceptance criteria:
- On handler failure (handler raises or returns False) store.last_audit_at_by_item is not updated (or last_success_at unchanged); last_attempt is recorded if implemented.
- On handler success, last_audit_at_by_item is updated to now.isoformat().
- Tests cover both failure and success paths.

Notes: This is the recommended semantics per intake.

workItems.title: Test: filter_by_cooldown behaviour
workItems.description:
Unit tests for _filter_by_cooldown:
- Candidate with last_audit less than cooldown -> excluded
- Candidate with last_audit exactly at boundary -> included
- Candidate with no store entry -> included

Acceptance: tests validate cooldown semantics described in audit_poller._filter_by_cooldown.

workItems.title: Test: select_candidate stable ordering & performance
workItems.description:
Unit test for _select_candidate refactor:
- Provide multiple candidates with/without updated_at and assert selected candidate is the oldest per rules.
- Ensure selection is stable and deterministic after refactor.

Acceptance: tests confirm identical pre/post semantics and no regressions.

audit.author: rgardler
audit.text:
Ready to close: Yes

## Summary

All acceptance criteria are satisfied. The implementation in src/ampa/audit_poller.py precomputes parsed timestamps once per candidate and sorts using the cached parsed values (avoiding repeated reparsing). Tests were added in tests/test_select_candidate.py that verify missing-updatedAt precedence, oldest-timestamp selection, deterministic tie-breaking (preserving input order), and include a lightweight micro-benchmark to detect performance regressions. The full test suite passed locally, so the item is ready to close.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | Provide multiple candidates with/without updated_at and assert selected candidate is the oldest per rules. | met | tests/test_select_candidate.py:14-24 — test_select_candidate_prefers_missing_updated_at_first; tests/test_select_candidate.py:27-36 — test_select_candidate_selects_oldest_timestamp. |
| 2 | Ensure selection is stable and deterministic after refactor (no regressions). | met | tests/test_select_candidate.py:40-54 — test_select_candidate_tie_breaks_stably_preserve_input_order; tests/test_select_candidate.py:57-72 — test_select_candidate_performance_microbenchmark; src/ampa/audit_poller.py:369-377 — parsed timestamps cached once per candidate and sort uses the cached values (stable sort preserves input order). |

## Children Status

No children.

workItems.title: Install: prefer src/ampa when copying AMPA package
workItems.description:
Modify the installer to prefer the repository-local src/ampa directory when copying the Python package into the target plugin location. This ensures the installer copies the full runtime instead of the minimal bundled stubs.

Acceptance criteria:
- Work item created and claimed.
- Installer patched and committed on a feature branch.
- Running the installer results in the installed plugin containing the full runtime (engine/ present) and daemon starts without ModuleNotFoundError for ampa.engine.*.
